mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 1-3 month basis, discount rates and funding stress ease, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS has declined for three consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent local trend signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and lower near-term equity earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the sign relation disappears after one-month labor deteriorations persist for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which should support equity prices over the next quarter as firms revise sales expectations upward.
failure_condition: Reject if higher monthly RSAFS growth does not correspond to higher subsequent 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips in a rolling 3-year window with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST remains weak, industrial output is improving faster than housing supply, suggesting a manufacturing-led rebound that should lift cyclicals and the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-minus-HOUST interaction has no positive predictive power for 20-day SPX returns, or if either variable alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE marks broad labor-market weakening, which should forecast negative S&P 500 returns because payroll income and consumption expectations deteriorate together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the joint condition adds no incremental signal beyond either labor series alone.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL inflation with a still-elevated FEDFUNDS rate implies restrictive real financing conditions; this combination should pressure equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate episodes fail to produce weaker 20-day SPX returns than baseline, or if the interaction term is not negative after controlling for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in HOUST signals collapsing residential investment and weaker downstream demand for durable goods, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if large housing contractions are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses sign during repeated housing drawdown episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary industrial slump, simultaneous weakness in INDPRO and PAYEMS identifies broad supply-side contraction and profit margin compression, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weakness of INDPRO and PAYEMS does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect remains after removing one of the two series from the interaction.
